concepts.domain: Board game cafe and tabletop retail brand (neighborhood storefront with online presence)
concepts.site_goal: Drive in-cafe reservations, event/tournament signups, and loyalty club registrations while letting visitors browse the 800-game library and menu before they arrive.
audience: Casual cafe-goers looking for a Friday night activity
Hobby-grade board gamers searching for rare titles in the library
Group organizers booking birthday parties or company offsites
concepts.description: Meeple & Mug is a multi-room board game cafe in a converted warehouse that doubles as a tabletop brand publishing two original card games. The website pairs a chunky illustrated style — hex-tile background patterns, meeple mascots in tangerine and electric purple, scribbled drop-shadows on buttons — with a confident utility layer: a searchable game library with filters for player count / play time / complexity / mood, a live event calendar with tournament brackets, a private room booking flow with deposit, a drinks-and-snacks menu styled like a vintage diner board with playful illustrations, and a loyalty Tavern Pass tier ladder. The blog reviews new releases each Friday with a rubric scorecard. The visual feel is candy-bright and tactile, like opening a colorful box of dice.
concepts.motif: Hex-tile background grids, chunky bordered illustration cards, meeple silhouettes used as bullet markers, dice-face section dividers, sticker-style price tags

pages.layout_pattern: Hero with oversized illustrated cafe scene, three feature cards (Tonight's Events / New on the Shelf / Book a Table), inline drinks teaser strip, loyalty signup band, latest blog reviews row, footer with hours and map
sections: Hero illustration with tagline Roll Sip Repeat
Tonight at the cafe — live event chips
Featured games of the month — 4-up card row
Drinks teaser with three signature cocktails

pages.layout_pattern: Diner-board-styled menu with four columns (Bites, Sweets, Sips, Spirits), each item as an illustrated chip-card with price tag sticker; allergen filter strip at top; staff pick callouts

Two-pane layout: left filter rail (player count slider, play time chips, complexity dots, mood tags, availability toggle), right card grid of game tiles with availability badge; sort dropdown; pagination
sections: Library hero with search bar What are you in the mood to play

pages.layout_pattern: Calendar grid with month/list toggle, event type legend, expandable tournament bracket modules below the calendar; featured weekly leagues sidebar
sections: Events hero with current month banner
Event type legend (Tournament / League / Learn-to-Play / Open Social)

pages.layout_pattern: Stepper-style booking flow: room selection card grid then date/time picker then add-ons then contact and deposit; sticky summary sidebar showing total
sections: Booking intro with three room illustrations (The Dragon's Den / The Hex Hall / The Quiet Corner)
Step 2: Pick date and party size
Step 3: Add-ons (snack platter, dedicated host, themed setup)

pages.layout_pattern: Tier ladder with three illustrated chunky tier cards (Pawn / Knight / Queen), perks comparison table, signup form, points-to-perks calculator
Editorial grid with large lead review card on top, three secondary cards below, rubric category filters, archive list at bottom
sections: Lead review card with rubric scorecard
Filter by rubric category (Strategy / Party / Solo / Family / Heavy)

message_intent: Communicate that the cafe is warm, social, and beginner-friendly — not a hushed hobby shop
Make the 800-game library feel browsable instead of overwhelming
Reduce friction on booking a table or private room
Position the in-house published games as part of a legitimate tabletop brand
Build a sense of repeat-visit ritual through the loyalty program
content_model: Game (title, designer, players, play time, complexity 1-5, mood tags, shelf location, currently checked out?)
Event (name, date, type: tournament/league/learn-to-play/open social, host, capacity, entry fee, prize pool)
Menu item (name, category: bites/sweets/drinks/booze, price, dietary tags, illustration)
Private room (capacity, hourly rate, included games, deposit amount, availability calendar)
Loyalty tier (name: Pawn/Knight/Queen, points threshold, perks list)
Blog review (game reviewed, rubric scores, reviewer, verdict, related games)
Staff guide (name, favorite game, signature drink, photo)
interactions: Filter game library by player count slider, play time chips, complexity dots, and mood tag pills
Live availability badge on each game card (On Shelf / In Play / Reserved)
Calendar grid toggle between Month and List view for events
Tournament bracket viewer that expands inline
Private booking flow: pick room then date/time then add-ons then deposit
Loyalty signup with progress meter and tier preview
Blog reviews filterable by rubric category and player count
asset_needs: Chunky illustrated meeple mascot family in tangerine and purple
Dice-face section divider set (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20)
Photo or illustration treatment for staff portraits framed in hex tiles
Cover thumbnails for about 12 sample games in the library
concepts.mobile_behavior: Hex-tile backgrounds switch to a softer two-tone wash on mobile to avoid pattern noise; game library filters collapse into a bottom-sheet drawer; event calendar defaults to list view; booking flow becomes a step-by-step single-column wizard with sticky CTA

concepts.domain: Tabletop gaming brand with attached game tavern and tournament league (publisher-first identity, cafe as flagship)
concepts.site_goal: Sell the Hex & Hops original game line, drive league registrations and tournament tickets, and convert visitors into Hex Crew loyalty members who book recurring tavern nights.
audience: Casual party-game buyers shopping the catalog
Parents booking the kids table on weekend afternoons
Friend groups reserving a private tavern booth
Designers and reviewers following the studio blog
concepts.description: Hex & Hops is a tabletop publisher with a single flagship tavern in the back of an old brewery. The brand publishes four signature games (a heavy strategy hex-builder, a party word game, a co-op dungeon crawler, a kids dice game) and runs the regional Hex League tournament circuit. The website leans into the brand's playful illustrated chunky aesthetic with a candy-bright palette — tangerine button fills, electric purple shadows, mint accents on hex-tile backgrounds, cream paper textures behind type. Sections feel like opening a game box: cards have die-cut chunky borders, icons look hand-carved, headers use a bold display sans with extra ink trapping. The site supports a game catalog with retailer locator, a tavern menu/booking page, a league/tournament calendar with brackets and standings, a private group booking flow, a Hex Crew loyalty signup with stamp-card UI, and a designer's blog with new release reviews and behind-the-scenes notes.
concepts.motif: Die-cut hex tiles with thick chunky borders, illustrated meeple-and-mug icon set, stamp-card visual for loyalty, brewery-style chalkboard menu rendering, league standings tables styled like scorecards

pages.layout_pattern: Hero with chunky illustrated game box stack and headline Pull up a chair, three-up feature row (Shop the Games / Visit the Tavern / Join the League), seasonal release spotlight, league standings teaser, Hex Crew stamp-card invite, blog row, footer with brewery address
sections: Hero illustration with stacked game boxes
Hex Crew loyalty band with stamp-card visual

pages.layout_pattern: Catalog grid of four flagship games with filter chips at top, each card opens into a detailed product page; sidebar showing retailer locator entry
Tavern menu and booking page combined: chalkboard-styled menu columns, illustrated floor plan with bookable booths, hours strip, booking CTA leading to flow
sections: Tavern hero with brewery photo treatment
Chalkboard menu — Bites / Pints / Pours / Sweets
Illustrated floor plan with bookable booths

pages.layout_pattern: Tournament hub: upcoming events calendar at top, current season standings table in the middle, expandable bracket viewer below, sign-up flow at bottom
sections: Hex League hero with season banner
Current standings sortable table with season switcher

pages.layout_pattern: Stepper booking flow with illustrated floor-plan booth picker, date/time picker, add-ons, deposit; right-side summary card
sections: Step 3: Add-ons (game host, snack platter, themed decor)

pages.layout_pattern: Loyalty page with full-width stamp-card hero, how-it-works steps, perks ladder, signup form, member spotlights
sections: Hex Crew hero with stamp-card visual
How it works — three illustrated steps

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial layout with category tabs (Reviews / Designer Diaries / Release News), lead post hero, secondary cards, author roster strip

message_intent: Establish Hex & Hops as a credible publisher, not just a cafe
Make the tavern feel like a community clubhouse for the league
Lower the barrier to learning the four signature games
Drive recurring visits via the stamp-card loyalty motif
Showcase upcoming releases through designer-led storytelling
content_model: Game title (cover art, players, age, play time, complexity, designer, price, retailer links, rulebook PDF)
Tavern menu item (name, type, price, illustration, pairing suggestion: best with [game])
League event (date, format, buy-in, prize, venue, registered players list, bracket)
Standing (player handle, season points, wins, current rank)
Private booking (booth or room, capacity, hourly rate, included games, deposit)
Loyalty stamp (visit type: tournament/booking/purchase, points value, perk unlocks)
Blog post (type: review/designer diary/release news, author, related game)
Designer (name, portrait, games credited, short bio)
interactions: Game catalog filter by player count, age, complexity, and good-for mood
Retailer locator with zip search and map
League standings sortable table with season switcher
Bracket viewer with expandable matches and final-score reveal
Tavern booking flow with booth picker on illustrated floor plan
Loyalty stamp-card visualization that fills as a member earns visits
Newsletter inline signup at the end of every blog post
Menu pairing toggle that highlights items recommended with a chosen game
asset_needs: Four chunky illustrated game box covers
Hex-tile section dividers with mint outline
Stamp-card SVG with twelve hex slots
Illustrated floor plan of the tavern showing booths and bar
Icon set: dice, mug, meeple, scroll, sword, crown
concepts.mobile_behavior: Hex-tile decorative patterns simplify to flat fills below 600px; the catalog filter rail collapses into a sheet; league standings tables become a card stack with rank, name, points; bracket viewer becomes a horizontally scrollable strip; the tavern floor plan switches to a list of booth cards
